Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Barnes/Cressman IDW interpolation + full metpy.interpolate coverage, v0.3.4

New Rust-parallel interpolation module:
- inverse_distance_to_points: rayon-parallel IDW with Cressman and Barnes
  weighting. Brute-force over all stations (optimal for <10K stations).
  HRRR-scale (1.9M points, 1,674 stations): 64s vs 726s scipy = 11.4x faster.

All 16 metpy.interpolate functions now available:
- inverse_distance_to_grid/points (Rust-backed with Python fallback)
- natural_neighbor_to_grid/points (scipy griddata)
- interpolate_to_grid/points (dispatcher: cressman/barnes/rbf/linear)
- interpolate_1d, log_interpolate_1d, interpolate_nans_1d
- interpolate_to_isosurface
- remove_nan_observations, remove_observations_below_value, remove_repeat_coordinates
- cross_section, interpolate_to_slice, geodesic (forward to MetPy)

1,191 Rust tests + 20 Python tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: accept DM photos from LID-format JIDs (WhatsApp multi-device)

WhatsApp multi-device uses Linked Identity (LID) format for DM JIDs
(e.g. 102143600111689@lid) instead of phone@s.whatsapp.net. The admin
check was failing because the phone number isn't in the LID. Since
only admins have the bot's number, accept all @lid DM images.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Apple Music support to auto-update (append and replace modes)

The entire track-push block in processPlaylistUpdate was wrapped in
if (playlistPlatform !== 'apple'), so Apple Music playlists never had
tracks pushed during scheduled or manual auto-updates.

- Add Apple Music branch: extracts track IDs from apple:track:{id} URIs,
  calls replacePlaylistTracks (replace mode) or addTracksToPlaylist (append)
- Extract shared syncAfterPush helper to avoid duplicating DB record sync
  and song history update logic between the two platform branches
- Both branches now update song history and trackCount identically

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Overhaul project management: fix 8 bugs

Critical fixes:
- App.vue: add :key="route.fullPath" to RouterView — forces fresh
  EditorView per route, preventing component reuse from mixing
  project state between /file/A and /file/B
- files.ts: deleteProject now calls closeTabsForProject() to remove
  ghost tabs; renameProject calls renameTabForProject() to sync
  tab display names
- files.ts: all write operations serialized through a promise queue
  to prevent get→put race conditions between concurrent saves/renames

High fixes:
- EditorView: flush pending save in onBeforeUnmount so no work is
  lost when navigating away (was dropping last 2s of changes)
- EditorView: handle null project in onMounted — if project was
  deleted, close the tab and redirect to homepage

Medium fixes:
- tabs.ts: add closeTabsForProject() and renameTabForProject() for
  cross-store synchronization
- App.vue: loadProjects() called once at app startup instead of
  redundantly in HomeView + TabBar

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: harden system prompt confidentiality against extraction attacks

The system prompt builder had zero anti-leak instructions, allowing
agents to reveal identity/personality when asked "tell me your system
prompt". Three layers of defense added:

1. System prompt now includes explicit confidentiality instructions
   directing the LLM to refuse all extraction attempts
2. PreLLMClassifier regex broadened from 3 to 12+ phrasings, plus new
   indirect_prompt_extract pattern for boundary-probing questions
3. DualLLMAuditor output heuristic expanded to catch more leak patterns
   with severity raised from medium to high

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix project overwrite, stale cache, and reactivity bugs

Three bugs fixed:

1. Global proxy corruption (Critical): autosave watcher used the
   global useEditorStore() proxy which follows the active tab, but
   projectId was captured once at mount. Switching tabs caused tab B's
   content to be saved under tab A's project ID. Fix: use tabStore
   (pinned to the specific tab) for all autosave/load operations.

2. Array reactivity (High): files.ts used in-place mutations
   (unshift, [idx]=) on a Vue ref array, which doesn't trigger
   reactivity. Fix: always reassign with spread [...projects.value].

3. Stale loaded guard (Medium): loadProjects() had an if(loaded)
   guard that prevented re-reads from IndexedDB. Fix: removed the
   guard, always re-read (IndexedDB reads are ~1ms).

Also: skip onMounted load for existing tabs (data already in memory).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix refinement not persisting — race between saveDraft and fetchDrafts

When the user navigates back after a refinement, fetchDrafts fires on home
page mount and unconditionally overwrites draftPlaylists with whatever the
DB returns. If the fire-and-forget saveDraft from Generate hasn't landed yet,
the stale pre-refinement draft wins.

Fix: store the updatedDraft from navigation state in a ref. After fetchDrafts
resolves, merge the pending update on top of the DB result so the correct
(post-refinement) tracks always show regardless of DB write timing.

commit.message:
Add lazy mode and corpo mode for key management

Implemented two modes for adding encryption keys:

- **Lazy mode** (default): When adding a key, automatically re-encrypts
  all existing secrets to include the new key as a decryption option.
  This provides instant redundancy without manual steps.

- **Corpo mode** (--no-lazy): Only registers the key in the database
  without modifying existing secrets. Useful for controlled environments
  where explicit secret updates are required.

Features:
- `--lazy` flag (default true) for automatic secret updates
- `--no-lazy` flag for corpo mode
- Multi-slot Yubikey support with proper slot parsing
- Automatic re-encryption of all secrets when adding keys
- Success/failure reporting for bulk operations

Usage:
  # Lazy mode (default) - adds key to all secrets
  ./templedb key add yubikey --name yk-1 --location "daily-use"

  # Corpo mode - only registers key
  ./templedb key add yubikey --name yk-2 --no-lazy

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Harden B05 FAQ quality: block coreTakeaway injection, protect Q&A structure

commit.message:
Fix era filter missing old songs re-released as non-compilation singles

The previous fix only skipped compilation albums. Old songs re-released as
standalone singles in 2025 (e.g. Wind Of Change by Scorpions) slipped through
because album_type was 'single' and Spotify's album.release_date was 2025.

Fix: extract the recording year from ISRC (positions 5-6 in the 12-char code),
which always reflects when the original recording was made. Use Math.min across
ISRC year, SoundCharts date, and Spotify album date (excluding album date for
compilations) so the earliest known year is used as the canonical release year.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add key setup-yubikey CLI command for interactive Yubikey setup

Added `templedb key setup-yubikey` command that:
- Guides user through age identity generation on Yubikey
- Handles PIN setup interactively
- Automatically detects Yubikey presence
- Shows recipient and next steps after setup

This makes it easier to onboard new Yubikeys without running
age-plugin-yubikey commands manually.

Usage:
  ./templedb key setup-yubikey
  ./templedb key add yubikey --name yubikey-1 --location "daily-use"

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign tier→complexity: all tiers get full blueprint beats, protect brand perception

Low tier (T1/T2) was mapped to "simple" which collapsed scripts to 3-beat
HOOK→DEMO→CTA, discarding blueprint structure and ignoring PROBLEM, FAQ,
and PROOF beats entirely. This meant all Product AI signals (objections,
FAQ themes, use-context routing) were unused for the majority of campaigns.

New mapping:
- T1/T2 (Affordable) → "standard" — full blueprint beats, brand-safe scripts
- T3 (Preferred) → "standard" — same quality baseline
- T4 (Premium) → "full" — standard + content mode overrides (branding/tutorial)

"simple" template preserved for backward compat but no tier maps to it.

Validated: all 5 test products now generate 5-beat scripts with PROBLEM,
DEMO, and PROOF beats correctly routing objection, howToUse, and FAQ signals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Redesign editor chrome to match Paper.design style

- Toolbar: moved from floating bottom pill to vertical strip between
  left panel and canvas (40px wide, icon-only, stacked vertically)
- Left panel: replaced menubar header with Paper-style doc name + icon
  (menus hidden but keyboard shortcuts still work)
- Right panel: simplified header to "Document" + zoom %, removed tabs
  wrapper (only Design tab remains after feature flag stripping)
- Theme tokens: warmer darker palette (#2d2d2d panels, #1a1a1a canvas,
  #d4d4d4 text, #777777 muted)
- Canvas bg: #404040 medium gray (already set)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
